title: The Artifact With a Signed Bloodline
tagline: Every version of the artifact cryptographically signs the one before it; snap the chain anywhere and the page names the exact generation where the bloodline breaks.
holy_shit: A one-file git with signatures and no server: each improvement pass signs the hash of the state before it, forming an append-only lineage the page re-verifies live — edit any ancestor and it points at the precise link that no longer signs, and hands that proof to a stranger as a QR.
powered_by: native crypto.subtle ECDSA P-256 (generateKey / sign / verify — a primitive no existing post uses) + SHA-256 + IndexedDB for the append-only chain + qr.mjs to re-emit the independently-verifiable head
agent_scenario: When you need tamper-evident provenance / version history with no server or ledger: have each new state sign SHA-256(state) ‖ the previous signature, persist the append-only chain locally, and let any holder of the public key walk it to pinpoint the exact generation where the lineage breaks — and re-verify a shared head from a QR alone.
author: kody-w + Claude
